## Freelance Services Agreement (Brand Refresh 2025)

### Preamble

This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of January 15, 2025 ("Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., through its Marketing Director Sarah Johnson ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, through its Senior Designer Alex Chen ("Freelancer"). Client and Freelancer may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

### Recitals

WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Freelancer to perform a complete visual identity redesign described as "Brand Refresh 2025," including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document; and

WHEREAS, Freelancer represents that Freelancer has the skill, experience, and ability to perform the services required by Client and is willing to perform the services under the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## Clauses (24 total)

### Services

*Type: standard*

Freelancer shall perform the services described in Exhibit A (the "Services") in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards. The Services shall include creation of logo concepts, refinement of selected logo, preparation of a brand guidelines document, and preparation and delivery of an asset package as set forth in the Deliverables section of this Agreement. Freelancer will provide the Services in accordance with the timeline and specifications set forth in this Agreement.

### Deliverables, Schedule and Milestones

*Type: standard*

Deliverables, due dates and amounts are as follows:

- Logo concepts (3 options): Due January 30, 2025 — $2,000
- Selected logo refinement: Due February 15, 2025 — $1,500
- Brand guidelines document: Due March 1, 2025 — $2,500
- Asset package delivery (final assets, file formats, source files as agreed): Due March 15, 2025 — $2,500

Client and Freelancer agree that the final Deadline for completion of all Deliverables is March 15, 2025. Time is of the essence. Freelancer shall use commercially reasonable efforts to meet delivery dates; however, delivery dates are contingent upon Client providing timely feedback, approvals and required materials as set forth in Section 6 (Client Responsibilities).

### Payment; Deposit; Application of Deposit; Invoices

*Type: standard*

Total Fee: The total fee for the Services shall be Eight Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (USD $8,500).

Deposit: Client shall pay a non-refundable deposit of Two Thousand Five Hundred Fifty Dollars (USD $2,550) upon execution of this Agreement. The deposit will be applied against the total fee as follows: the deposit will be credited to the earliest outstanding milestone invoices in chronological order.

Milestone Payments: After application of the deposit, Freelancer will invoice Client for each milestone upon delivery of the corresponding Deliverable and Client's acceptance (or upon expiration of the acceptance period under Section 7). Each invoice is due within seven (7) calendar days of invoice date unless otherwise agreed in writing.

Late Payments: Any amounts not paid when due shall accrue interest at the rate of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) or the maximum rate allowed by applicable law, whichever is lower, from the due date until paid. Client shall be responsible for all reasonable costs of collection, including court costs and attorneys' fees, to the extent allowed by applicable law and as provided in Section 15 (Attorneys' Fees).

### Revision Rounds and Acceptance

*Type: standard*

Included Revisions: Client is entitled to three (3) rounds of revisions for each Deliverable as part of the Fees. A "revision round" means a single consolidated request for changes submitted by Client following delivery of a Deliverable. Additional revisions beyond the included rounds will be billed at a rate of One Hundred Fifty Dollars (USD $150) per hour or at a mutually agreed fixed fee.

Acceptance: Upon delivery of each Deliverable, Client shall have seven (7) business days to review and either (a) accept the Deliverable in writing, or (b) provide consolidated written comments requesting revisions. If Client does not respond within seven (7) business days, the Deliverable will be deemed accepted and Freelancer will be entitled to invoice for the applicable milestone.

### Client Responsibilities; Materials Provided by Client

*Type: standard*

Client shall provide timely feedback, approvals, source materials, brand assets, content, and any other information or access reasonably necessary for Freelancer to perform the Services. Freelancer is not responsible for delays caused by Client's failure to provide required items. Client represents and warrants that any materials provided to Freelancer do not infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party and that Client has the right to provide such materials to Freelancer for use in the Services.

### Intellectual Property Ownership and Assignment

*Type: standard*

Ownership: Subject to full and final payment of all fees due under this Agreement, Freelancer hereby assigns to Client all right, title and interest in and to the final Deliverables created specifically for Client under this Agreement, including copyrights and other intellectual property rights therein ("Assigned IP").

Freelancer Rights: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Freelancer shall retain the right to (a) use the Deliverables or portions thereof within Freelancer's portfolio and promotional materials; and (b) retain pre-existing intellectual property, design tools, software, templates, stock materials, techniques, concepts, and know-how that are not unique to or exclusively derived from Client's Deliverables. To the extent any Deliverable includes Freelancer's pre-existing work or third-party materials, Client is granted only the license rights necessary to use such items as part of the Deliverables, and Freelancer will identify such items where practicable.

Assignment Process: If, by operation of law or otherwise, the Deliverables cannot be assigned as requested above, Freelancer agrees to grant to Client an exclusive, perpetual, worldwide, transferable license to use, reproduce, display, distribute, prepare derivative works of and otherwise exploit the Deliverables without additional compensation. Transfer of ownership is conditioned upon Client's payment in full of all amounts due under this Agreement.

Moral Rights: To the extent permitted by law, Freelancer hereby waives and agrees not to assert any moral rights or rights of attribution or integrity in the Assigned IP. Freelancer shall, upon Client's reasonable request and expense, execute such further instruments and take such steps as may be reasonably necessary to effectuate the intent of this Section.

### License Pending Payment; Use Prior to Final Payment

*Type: standard*

Until full payment is received, Freelancer retains ownership of the Deliverables. Upon delivery and prior to full payment, Freelancer grants Client a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable license to use the Deliverables solely for internal review and evaluation. Any other use of the Deliverables prior to full payment is prohibited without Freelancer's prior written consent. If Client uses the Deliverables prior to full payment without consent, Client shall be liable for the remaining balance and any damages resulting from such use.

### Confidentiality

*Type: standard*

Each Party ("Receiving Party") shall hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party any confidential or proprietary information disclosed by the other Party ("Disclosing Party") that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure ("Confidential Information"). Confidential Information shall not include information that is (a) publicly available without breach of this Agreement, (b) rightfully received from a third party without restriction, (c) independently developed without use of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, or (d) required to be disclosed by law or court order (provided the Receiving Party gives the Disclosing Party prior notice where practicable).

Duration: The confidentiality obligations shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years, except that trade secrets shall remain subject to protection for as long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.

### Representations and Warranties

*Type: standard*

Each Party represents and warrants that it has the full right, power and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform its obligations hereunder.

Freelancer represents and warrants that (a) the Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards; and (b) to Freelancer's knowledge, the Deliverables, as delivered to Client, will not infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party, except for third-party elements disclosed to Client.

Client represents and warrants that any materials provided to Freelancer do not infringe third-party rights and that Client has authority to grant the rights granted in this Agreement.

EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED ABOVE, THE SERVICES AND DELIVERABLES ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" AND FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT, TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW.

### Indemnification

*Type: standard*

Client shall indemnify, defend and hold Freelancer and its officers, agents and employees harmless from and against any and all claims, liabilities, damages, losses and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or in connection with any claim that materials provided by Client infringe a third party's intellectual property rights or otherwise violate applicable law.

Freelancer shall indemnify, defend and hold Client and its officers, agents and employees harmless from and against any and all claims, liabilities, damages, losses and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of Freelancer's gross negligence, willful misconduct, or material breach of Freelancer's representations and warranties herein, including any claim of direct infringement attributable to Freelancer's original work in the Deliverables.

### Limitation of Liability

*Type: standard*

IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, SPECIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT OR THE SERVICES, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), STRICT LIABILITY OR OTHERWISE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

EXCEPT FOR A PARTY'S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS OR LIABILITY ARISING FROM A PARTY'S GROSS NEGLIGENCE OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, EACH PARTY'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNTS PAID OR OWED BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

### Termination

*Type: standard*

Either Party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other Party that is not cured within fourteen (14) days after written notice specifying the breach. Client may terminate for convenience upon seven (7) days' written notice, in which case Client shall pay Freelancer for Services performed and Deliverables delivered through the effective date of termination, plus any reasonable costs incurred by Freelancer in winding down the Services. The deposit is non-refundable except where Freelancer materially fails to perform and such failure is not cured within the cure period.

Effect of Termination: Upon termination, Freelancer shall deliver to Client all Deliverables completed and any work-in-progress for which Client has paid. All sections that by their nature survive termination (including Intellectual Property, Confidentiality, Indemnification, Limitation of Liability, Payment and Governing Law) shall remain in effect.

### Independent Contractor

*Type: standard*

Freelancer is an independent contractor and is not an employee, agent, partner or joint venturer of Client. Freelancer shall be solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, workers' compensation insurance, and other statutory obligations in connection with the Services. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create an employment relationship.

### Insurance

*Type: optional*

Freelancer maintains general professional liability and errors & omissions insurance consistent with industry practice. Upon Client's written request, Freelancer shall provide proof of insurance for reasonable coverage amounts. The Parties acknowledge that insurance does not limit Freelancer's contractual obligations under this Agreement.

### Dispute Resolution; Governing Law; Venue

*Type: standard*

Governing Law; Venue: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The Parties hereby submit to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the state and federal courts located in San Francisco County, California for any disputes that are not subject to mandatory arbitration under this Section.

Dispute Resolution: The Parties shall first attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through informal negotiations between senior representatives. If the Parties cannot resolve the dispute informally within thirty (30) days, they shall attempt to resolve the dispute through mediation in San Francisco County, California under JAMS or another mutually agreed mediator. If mediation does not resolve the dispute within sixty (60) days of the mediator's appointment, the dispute shall be resolved by binding arbitration administered by JAMS in San Francisco County, California under the JAMS Streamlined Arbitration Rules and Procedures (or other mutually agreed rules). Judgment on the award rendered by the arbitrator may be entered in any court having jurisdiction.

Small Claims: Either Party may elect to proceed in small claims court for claims within the jurisdictional limits of that court.

### Force Majeure

*Type: standard*

Neither Party shall be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement (excluding payment obligations) due to causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, fire, flood, earthquake, pandemic, acts of government, war, terrorism, labor disputes, or failures of suppliers or internet service providers, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice and uses reasonable efforts to resume performance as soon as practicable.

### Notices

*Type: standard*

All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by personal delivery, nationally recognized overnight courier, certified mail (return receipt requested), or by email with read receipt to the addresses set forth below or to such other address as a Party may designate in writing. Notices shall be effective upon receipt or, in the case of email, upon confirmation of receipt.

Notices to Client: Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director, TechStartup Inc. [Client's mailing address and contact email to be inserted]

Notices to Freelancer: Alex Chen, Senior Designer, Chen Creative Studio [Freelancer's mailing address and contact email to be inserted]

### Publicity

*Type: optional*

Freelancer may include Client's name and the Deliverables in marketing materials and portfolio, subject to Client's reasonable guidelines and except where Client designates certain materials as confidential. Client grants Freelancer a non-exclusive right to use Client's name and logo solely to identify Client as a client of Freelancer unless Client provides written objection.

### Amendment; Waiver

*Type: standard*

This Agreement may only be amended or modified by a written instrument executed by both Parties. No waiver of any term or breach of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the waiving Party. Failure to enforce any provision shall not constitute a waiver of future enforcement.

### Severability; Entire Agreement; Assignment

*Type: standard*

Severability: If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

Entire Agreement: This Agreement, together with any exhibits or attachments, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, proposals and communications, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter.

Assignment: Client may not assign this Agreement or assign or transfer any rights hereunder without Freelancer's prior written consent; provided, however, that Client may assign this Agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition or sale of substantially all of its assets. Freelancer may not assign the Agreement except with Client's written consent, except that Freelancer may assign to an affiliate or in connection with a sale of Freelancer's business.

### Record Retention; Source Files

*Type: standard*

Freelancer shall retain copies of project files for a reasonable period (not less than one year) following final delivery. Unless otherwise agreed in writing, delivery of an asset package shall include native files (e.g., AI, PSD), export files (PNG, JPG, SVG, PDF) and font files only where Freelancer has the right to distribute such fonts. Client is responsible for obtaining any font licenses required for distribution or modification beyond the intended uses.

### Counterparts; Execution

*Type: standard*

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures (including scanned signatures and signatures executed via electronic signature platforms) shall be binding and enforceable to the same extent as handwritten signatures to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law.

### Exhibits

*Type: standard*

Exhibit A: Description of Services and Deliverables (as set forth in Section 2) and Project Timeline

- Project: Brand Refresh 2025
- Description: Complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and brand guidelines document
- Effective Date: January 15, 2025
- Deadline: March 15, 2025
- Deliverables and Due Dates:
  - Logo concepts (3 options) — Due January 30, 2025 — $2,000
  - Selected logo refinement — Due February 15, 2025 — $1,500
  - Brand guidelines document — Due March 1, 2025 — $2,500
  - Asset package delivery — Due March 15, 2025 — $2,500
- Payment Summary:
  - Total Fee: $8,500
  - Deposit (due on execution): $2,550 (non-refundable, applied to earliest invoices)
  - Remaining balance invoiced per milestone upon acceptance or deemed acceptance (7 business days)
- Revision Rounds: 3 included per Deliverable

Any changes to scope, timeline, deliverables or fees must be documented in a written amendment signed by both Parties.

### Attorney's Fees

*Type: standard*

If any action (including arbitration) is instituted to enforce any provision of this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees, court costs and other reasonable expenses in addition to any other relief to which that Party may be entitled, to the extent permitted by applicable law.
